content.full_text:
September 15, 1976


Mrs. Kenneth Fleck, Treasurer
Laguna Niguel Republican Women's Club
407 Monarch Bay
So. Laguna, California 92677

Dear Mrs. Fleck:

This letter is in response to your inquiry of August 30, 1976, concerning the reporting obligations of the Laguna Niguel Republican Women's Club under the Political Reform Act.

A group which receives contributions or makes expenditures of $500 or more during a calendar year for the purpose of influencing the action of voters is a committee under the Act. In regulations 2 Cal. Adm. Code 18215 and 18225, copies of which are enclosed, the Commission defined the terms "contribution" and "expenditure." Funds received by political clubs such as the Laguna Niguel Republican Women's Club are "contributions" only to the extent that the funds are "earmarked" for the making of contributions or expenditures. Funds are "earmarked" if the donor knows or has reason to know that the funds will be used to support candidates. Since the club did not contribute to campaigns at all in 1975 and has not received or made contributions of $500 during 1976, it is not a committee and is not required to file campaign statements. See Opinion requested by Francis Wilmarth, 2 FPPC Opinions 130 (August 3, 1976), copy enclosed.

If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,


Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

Enclosure

bcc: Jeanne P.

# [The text in this document is printed/typed upside down and backwards, making accurate transcription extremely difficult. The document appears to be a multi-page letter or document on pink paper with two white fasteners at the top. There is also a red mark or stamp at the very top of the page.]

Due to the reversed and inverted orientation of the text combined with the image quality, a reliable word-for-word transcription cannot be accurately provided without significant risk of error. The document would need to be photographed or scanned in the correct orientation to transcribe its contents accurately.

FPPC
Seq 1 9 JUN'76

August 30, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "U" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

We are the Laguna Miguel Republican Women's Club. Our current
membership is 104 regular members and 45 associate members. This
is a request for a letter of advice. Please determine our filing
status since the Political Reform Act took effect in 1975. The
following information is supplied to inform you about our club:

-On September 6, 1974 before the Political Reform Act
took effect, we were assigned an identification #7044236.

With regard to 1975:

1. Our club did not make any contributions to or incur
expenditures for any persons who were holding or campaigning
for any elective office—state, county, federal, judicial,
district or municipal.

2. The source of our funds received during 1975 was:
   a. Dues: $3.00 per regular member and $5.00 per
      associate member. The total dues received
      during 1975 were $656.00.
   b. Donations: $12.00 was received from donations
      from members during 1975.
   c. Other: The only other sources of incoming funds
      during 1975 were $8.00 profit from a Bridge Luncheon,
      $108.50 from a Christmas Bazaar and $12.00 miscellaneous.

3. The expenditures made by our club totaled $405.03. The
majority of our expenditures were for printing a membership
roster, printing and mailing a monthly meeting notice,
expenses incurred from an annual membership tea and payment
of dues to the Council of Republican Women($12.50). As
indicated under #1 above, none of the 1975 expenditures
were for persons holding or campaigning for elective office.

[illegible] US Census

3997
mailed [illegible]

[Most of the document content is severely faded and illegible. There appear to be multiple paragraphs of text, but the words are too faint to read accurately. The document appears to be a typed letter or official correspondence with several paragraphs and what might be a signature or closing at the bottom.]

[Due to the poor quality and faded nature of this document, I cannot reliably transcribe the specific text content without risking significant errors. The image shows what appears to be a multi-paragraph typed document with a red stamp or marking at the top, but the actual text is too illegible to transcribe accurately.]

-2-

With regard to 1976:

-Our sources of income will be about the same. About $550.will
come in from membership dues. In addition, we will have income
from a Boutique and a Bridge Luncheon.
-Our expenditures will be about the same as 1975.
-In 1976 the substantial difference will be that the club will
probably make contributions to persons campaigning for
elective office. The grand total of such contributions
will not exceed $500.

The whole purpose in writing is to clarify our filing status. We
have been under the impression, after a lengthy conversation with the
have been under the impression, after a lengthy conversation with the
Secretary of State's office in August, 1975 that we were not required to
file, but we want to make sure we are correct and are complying with
file. At that time, I was advised to file a "Committee Report
Called on a law. At that time, I was advised to file a "Committee Report
of No Activity", which I did. When a question recently came up concerning
this, I talked with the Political Reform Division in Sacramento and was
told it did not appear that we had to file butwas advised to write to this
Commission for clarification.

In the event you conclude that it is necessary for us to file, please
be advised that we have not filed since August 4, 1975 when the above
mentioned report was filed. Please advise how our organization should
file for the past period, what form would be used and at what dates
filing should be made. Also, what form should be used for filing for
the remainder of this year should be made and at what date.
a grand total of $500 be made and at what date.

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Mrs. Kenneth Fleck
Treasurer
Laguna Niguel Republican Women's Club

Address:
407 Monarch Bay
La Laguna California 92677

[The image shows a heavily redacted or faded document with a red mark at the top. Most of the text is illegible or too faint to read accurately. The document appears to be typed on a typewriter, but the content is not clear enough to transcribe with confidence.]
